feed.title: Eirms | HuHeXian's Blog
feed.subtitle: A lifelong learner, a university student.
feed.rights: All rights reserved 2022, Eirms | HuHeXian's Blog

entry.title: Lost everything to get freedom
entry.content:
Recently, I has been watching a movie named Fight Club, there is a sentence that impressed me, "It's only after we've lost everything that we're free to do anything."

Think it carefully, the condition and result of this sentence is hard to achieve, it is an ideal situation. We can't lost everything, and we are unable to get absolute freedom. Firstly, I don't understand it until one day when I was in the shower and remembered an incident from ten years ago.

In 2010, with the help of my uncle, I successfully registered my first QQ account when QQ was so popular. There were some people who made efforts to upgrade level quickly and decorate their own QQ Show......Because it was my first time to use QQ, I was so interested in it that I thought it was important to me. One day, I found I can't login my QQ account, did someone steal my account? I tried my best to make my account back, but I failed. I was so sad, because there were many things that I thought important on QQ, such as the game data of Roco kindom, and my QQ friends. Although I would register a new one, I was still sad. However, luckily, I found my account, I just forgot the password, and now, I has been using it.

At that time, was the QQ number a kind of bondage for me? If I hadn't had it, I probably wouldn't have been so upset for so long about all the games, levels and other virtual things that I couldn't abandon in a sense. I don't think it would have mattered if I had cancelled my QQ number now.

The more you carry on your back, the harder it is to walk. The ideal state is to be relaxed and absolutely free, but reality does not give us absolute freedom, we cannot leave everything behind, there are many attachments and ties, we can only approach this ideal state step by step.

We can only move closer to this ideal state one step at a time. People often talk about "cutting off", cutting off all those things that are unnecessary, inappropriate and outdated, and cutting off our attachment to them, so that we can live a simple and refreshing life after "cutting off".

I look at the hundreds of apps installed on my phone, but what I actually use every day are only social apps such as WeChat and Weibo, and a few apps related to study and work, but why do I still have so many installed? Because we don't know when we'll use it next, so it's better to keep it in there than to install it again when the time comes.

It's a good idea to empty your mind of the things you need to leave behind and keep the people and things you want to treasure.

entry.title: Writing is about expressing what you truly feel
entry.summary: I have seen many people who are worried about nothing to write, in my views, the first point of writing is to express your true feelings, don't care about how well you write.
entry.content:
I have seen many people who are worried about nothing to write, in my views, the first point of writing is to express your true feelings, don't care about how well you write.

I believe that an article filled with true feelings and correct grammar will be popular. People like to read an article written by a flesh-and-blood person, not one cobbled together for traffic and profit.

There is a word: People are willing to follow a person, not a company. They have different aims about writing, the former is about expressing his or her true feelings and thoughts, and the latter usually aims to attain more traffic and profit.

Since I started writing on my blog, I have been encouraging those around me and those I have met online to be bold in expressing their views and ideas. Because I think that we need to think independently to anti those unfair things.

Believe in yourself, we can write invaluable articles.

entry.title: My first article on this blog made by Gridea
entry.summary:
How did I make this blog?

😁Good evening, everyone.

📝This is my first post on this blog made by Gridea. Everything is free except for this domain name, which I paid for, so this is almost a zero cost blog. If you want to make a blog like me, I highly recommend you use Gridea, it is an easy to use program and there are many tutorials online to help you use it.
entry.content:
How did I make this blog?

😁Good evening, everyone.

📝This is my first post on this blog made by Gridea. Everything is free except for this domain name, which I paid for, so this is almost a zero cost blog. If you want to make a blog like me, I highly recommend you use Gridea, it is an easy to use program and there are many tutorials online to help you use it.

Why did I make this blog?

🙋‍♂️As you know, I already have a blog, built using WordPress, but it is geared towards Chinese people and is a Chinese blog. I would like to build an English blog for posting my articles written in English. On the one hand, I can improve my English writing skills, and on the other hand, I hope to meet foreign friends through it and communicate and learn with them.

👉I wanted to build a blog with minimal cost and gridea was my first choice.

📌Gridea is a static blog writing client developed by EryouHao. I used it with GitHub Pages and it worked perfectly to build a static blog with a domain name purchased from namesilo.

What will I write on this blog?

👀Because I have limited energy, I may not post as often.

🎈I will be posting my thoughts and reflections on this blog, based on my level of English writing, and of course, I hope that in the future, my level of English will allow me to write fluently in English.

You can follow me here

🌐Twitter: huhexian
Ⓜ️Telegram: huhexian

I will continue to update about myself in a couple of days so that those who read this blog can get to know me more fully.
